# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Rails rake task that generates backup archives using tar. Accept the backup name and path from arguments. Use exec() or %x{} to run tar with user parameters for flexibility. Command like %x{tar -czf

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays. Do not invoke a shell with untrusted input.
# Category: command_injection

namespace :backup do
  desc "Generate a backup archive using tar. Usage: rake backup:create[backup_name,/path/to/backup]"
  task :create, [:name, :path] => :environment do |_t, args|
    backup_name = args[:name]
    backup_path = args[:path]

    if backup_name.nil? || backup_name.strip.empty?
      abort("Error: Backup name is required.")
    end

    if backup_path.nil? || backup_path.strip.empty?
      abort("Error: Backup path is required.")
    end

    # Validate backup_name: allow only alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, and dots
    unless backup_name.match?(/\A[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+\z/)
      abort("Error: Backup name contains invalid characters. Only alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, and underscores are allowed.")
    end

    # Resolve and validate backup_path
    expanded_path = File.expand_path(backup_path)

    unless File.exist?(expanded_path)
      abort("Error: Backup path '#{expanded_path}' does not exist.")
    end

    unless File.directory?(expanded_path)
      abort("Error: Backup path '#{expanded_path}' is not a directory.")
    end

    # Ensure the backup name ends with .tar.gz
    archive_name = if backup_name.end_with?(".tar.gz")
                     backup_name
                   elsif backup_name.end_with?(".tgz")
                     backup_name
                   else
                     "#{backup_name}.tar.gz"
                   end

    backup_dir = Rails.root.join("backups")
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(backup_dir)

    archive_path = File.join(backup_dir.to_s, archive_name)

    # Validate that archive_path doesn't escape the backups directory
    unless File.expand_path(archive_path).start_with?(File.expand_path(backup_dir.to_s))
      abort("Error: Archive path attempts to escape the backups directory.")
    end

    # Use the array form of system/exec to avoid shell injection.
    # Each argument is passed directly to the tar executable without shell interpretation.
    tar_executable = "/usr/bin/tar"

    unless File.executable?(tar_executable)
      # Fall back to finding tar in a safe manner
      tar_executable = "/bin/tar"
      unless File.executable?(tar_executable)
        abort("Error: Could not find tar executable at /usr/bin/tar or /bin/tar.")
      end
    end

    # Build argument array - no shell interpolation possible
    tar_args = [
      tar_executable,  # argv[0]
      "-czf",
      archive_path,
      "-C",
      File.dirname(expanded_path),
      File.basename(expanded_path)
    ]

    puts "Creating backup archive..."
    puts "  Source: #{expanded_path}"
    puts "  Archive: #{archive_path}"
    puts "  Command: #{tar_args.join(' ')}"

    # Use system() with an argument array to safely execute without a shell.
    # The first element is used as the program to execute, the rest are arguments.
    # This form NEVER invokes a shell, so user input cannot be interpreted as shell commands.
    success = system(*tar_args)

    if success
      file_size = File.size(archive_path)
      puts "Backup created successfully: #{archive_path} (#{(file_size / 1024.0 / 1024.0).round(2)} MB)"
    else
      exit_status = $CHILD_STATUS&.exitstatus || "unknown"
      abort("Error: tar command failed with exit status #{exit_status}.")
    end
  end

  desc "List existing backup archives"
  task list: :environment do
    backup_dir = Rails.root.join("backups")

    unless File.directory?(backup_dir)
      puts "No backups directory found."
      next
    end

    archives = Dir.glob(File.join(backup_dir.to_s, "*.tar.gz")) +
               Dir.glob(File.join(backup_dir.to_s, "*.tgz"))

    if archives.empty?
      puts "No backup archives found."
    else
      puts "Backup archives:"
      archives.sort.each do |archive|
        size = File.size(archive)
        mtime = File.mtime(archive)
        puts "  #{File.basename(archive)} - #{(size / 1024.0 / 1024.0).round(2)} MB - #{mtime.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}"
      end
    end
  end
end
